jokes.text: Remember, Bart... I mean, Dances in Underwear.
jokes.setup: Adult trying to use Native American names in children's play
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous made-up name 'Dances in Underwear'
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor with economical delivery and memorable phrasing

jokes.text: Keep your eyes peeled for Injuns. I mean, Native Americans.
jokes.setup: Child playing cowboys and Indians
jokes.punchline: Self-correcting to politically correct term mid-game
jokes.explanation: Good social satire about political correctness creeping into children's play

jokes.text: They're after us because we gave them blankets infected with cooties.
jokes.setup: Historical reference to smallpox blankets
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent substitution of 'cooties' for deadly disease
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that turns historical atrocity into child-appropriate terms while maintaining the essence

jokes.text: I ate all my caps.
jokes.setup: Children playing with toy guns that use caps
jokes.punchline: Child casually admits to eating ammunition
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic absurdity delivered with matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: That's not fair, Nelson. They didn't have the Killmatic 3000 back then.
jokes.setup: Children trying to play historically accurate cowboys
jokes.punchline: Complaint about anachronistic super-weapon with absurd name
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist product name and kid logic about fairness in pretend games

jokes.text: Hey, records from that era are spotty at best.
jokes.setup: Complaint about anachronistic weapon
jokes.punchline: Nelson's pseudo-intellectual defense using historical uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Nelson showing unexpected pseudo-sophistication while cheating at a children's game

jokes.text: Why the crap do we have to go church anyway?
jokes.setup: Family heading to church
jokes.punchline: Bart's mild profanity in complaint about church
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart irreverence, perfectly in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You just answered your own question with that commode mouth.
jokes.setup: Bart's mild profanity
jokes.punchline: Marge's euphemistic scolding using 'commode mouth'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge-ism - overly polite euphemism that's somehow more graphic than the original mild curse

jokes.text: And with flaming swords, the Aramites did pierce the eyes of their fellow men... and did feast on what flowed forth.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining kids need to learn morals and love
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by extremely violent biblical passage
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition comedy - violent biblical content immediately after talk of teaching love and morals

jokes.text: I'm a troll man
jokes.setup: Serious biblical reading in church
jokes.punchline: Child's troll doll apparently speaking during sermon
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag disrupting solemn moment, gets mild laugh but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I don't want you playing with something that has such bizarre hair. Awful, awful hair.
jokes.setup: Marge taking away troll doll
jokes.punchline: Marge criticizing hair while having the most distinctive hair on TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect character irony - Marge with her iconic blue beehive criticizing troll doll's hair

jokes.text: Wow, there is a God.
jokes.setup: Reverend introducing his attractive daughter Jessica
jokes.punchline: Bart's instant conversion upon seeing pretty girl
jokes.explanation: Classic hormone-driven revelation joke, perfectly economical and quotable

jokes.text: I'm telling you, the light would work better if it pointed out to sea! / Shut up, I know what I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Background workers during church scene
jokes.punchline: Arguing about lighthouse pointing inland
jokes.explanation: Good background gag typical of Simpsons - absurd municipal incompetence

jokes.text: I hate the sea and everything in it.
jokes.setup: Lighthouse argument
jokes.punchline: Worker's irrational hatred explaining backwards lighthouse
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist motivation for incompetence, very economical

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Bart Simpson. I was incredibly moved by your reading. I don't think God's words have ever sounded so plausible.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to impress Jessica with sophistication
jokes.punchline: Accidentally implying the Bible usually sounds implausible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - trying to be smooth but revealing his irreverence

jokes.text: Thanks, Art.
jokes.setup: Bart's elaborate introduction and compliment
jokes.punchline: Jessica gets his name wrong and dismisses him
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Bart's attempt to impress, economical rejection

jokes.text: She's a sweet, kind reverend's daughter, and you're the devil's cabana boy.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining why Bart and Jessica are incompatible
jokes.punchline: The absurd and specific description 'devil's cabana boy'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, original insult with perfect specificity and rhythm. One of the episode's best lines.

jokes.text: Ralph, Jesus did not have wheels.
jokes.setup: Sunday school art class
jokes.punchline: Ralph's characteristically bizarre interpretation of Jesus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum moment - innocent child logic taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: But, Bart, we banned you from Sunday school. You were happy. We were happy. Everybody was happy. Particularly the hamster.
jokes.setup: Bart requesting to return to Sunday school
jokes.punchline: Escalating list ending with mysterious hamster reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with mysterious final element suggesting past Bart mischief

jokes.text: What's prodigal mean?
jokes.setup: Teacher using Biblical reference to welcome back Bart
jokes.punchline: Students not understanding the reference they're supposed to be learning
jokes.explanation: Standard educational satire - kids not learning what they're supposed to learn

jokes.text: Must fight Satan.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to resist the David's slingshot in Sunday school
jokes.punchline: Internal monologue treating minor mischief as epic battle
jokes.explanation: Good internal monologue humor showing Bart's epic framing of minor temptation

jokes.text: Do you ever think anything you don't say?
jokes.setup: Bart constantly announcing his good intentions
jokes.punchline: Jessica's cutting observation about his lack of filter
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical putdown that reveals both characters perfectly

jokes.text: Now, the kilt was only for day-to-day wear. In battle, we donned a full-length ball gown covered in sequins. The idea was to blind your opponent with luxury.
jokes.setup: Willie teaching about Scottish military history
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated explanation involving ball gowns as battle gear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Willie moment - completely absurd historical 'fact' delivered with total conviction

jokes.text: Now, the kilt was only for day-to-day wear. In battle, we donned a full-length ball gown covered in sequins. The idea was to blind your opponent with luxury.
jokes.setup: Willie giving Scottish history lesson
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous claims about Scottish battle dress
jokes.explanation: Fantastic escalating absurdity perfectly suited to Willie's character

jokes.text: 'Tis no more than what God gave me, you Puritan pukes.
jokes.setup: Willie apparently exposing himself
jokes.punchline: His defiant justification with period-appropriate insult
jokes.explanation: Good Willie character moment with creative historical insult

jokes.text: Congratulations, Simpson. You just fell for our sting and won yourself three months' detention. There's no such thing as Scotchtoberfest!
jokes.setup: Bart enjoying Willie's Scottish history
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Scotchtoberfest was fake sting operation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection reveal with absurd made-up holiday name

jokes.text: You used me, Skinner! You used me!
jokes.setup: Bart discovering the sting operation
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top betrayal reaction to minor school discipline
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart melodrama, gets the job done but not exceptional

jokes.text: No, it's not that. Didn't you hear? They have no bananas. They have no bananas today.
jokes.setup: Marge thinking Homer is emotional about Bart's first date
jokes.punchline: Homer completely oblivious, focused on old novelty song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely missing emotional significance for trivial concern

jokes.text: Well, in my family, grades aren't that important. It's what you learn that counts. Six times five! What is it?
jokes.setup: Bart claiming his family values learning over grades
jokes.punchline: Reverend immediately exposing his ignorance with simple math
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff exposing Bart's pretensions immediately

jokes.text: Actually, numbers don't have much use in my future career: Olympic gold-medal rocket-sled champ!
jokes.setup: Bart caught not knowing basic math
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous made-up Olympic sport to justify ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good Bart improvisation with absurd specificity

jokes.text: Well, no offense, but what you don't know could fill a warehouse.
jokes.setup: Reverend expressing skepticism about rocket-sled Olympics
jokes.punchline: Bart's inadvertently self-defeating comeback
jokes.explanation: Good ironic comeback that works against Bart given the context

jokes.text: Never have I heard such gratuitous use of the word 'butt'! / But... But... But... / Make him stop!
jokes.setup: Reverend outraged by Bart's story with word 'butt'
jokes.punchline: Bart's attempt to defend himself just repeats the offensive word
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay gag with good escalation structure

jokes.text: Never have I heard such gratuitous use of the word 'butt'!
jokes.setup: Bart apparently telling inappropriate story at dinner
jokes.punchline: Reverend's shocked reaction to mild profanity
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to unseen inappropriate story, shows character difference in standards

jokes.text: Smart, beautiful and a liar. So much better than that Sarah, plain and tall.
jokes.setup: Jessica revealing her deceptive nature
jokes.punchline: Bart's twisted attraction to her dishonesty and literary reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Bart's attraction to danger plus clever literary reference

jokes.text: It's like the toilet paper is an extension of your body.
jokes.setup: Bart praising Jessica's toilet papering skills
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top romantic admiration for vandalism technique
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist romantic praise for destructive behavior, very Bart

jokes.text: That's for besmirching an innocent girl's name.
jokes.setup: Kid hitting Bart for claiming to date Jessica
jokes.punchline: Using formal vocabulary 'besmirching' in playground violence
jokes.explanation: Good vocabulary mismatch humor, kid using fancy word for simple concept

jokes.text: Actually, if the road surface maintains its integrity... I just might be able to hold on.
jokes.setup: Bart on dangerous skateboard ride
jokes.punchline: Suddenly using engineering terminology in moment of panic
jokes.explanation: Good vocabulary incongruity for Bart's character in crisis moment

jokes.text: The glue will slow me down. Any glue leaking? No. That glue ain't going nowhere.
jokes.setup: Bart hoping glue truck will slow his dangerous skateboard ride
jokes.punchline: Worker confirming glue is secure right before it spills
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure with dramatic irony

jokes.text: Have you noticed any change in Bart? New glasses? No. He looks like something might be disturbing him. Probably misses his old glasses.
jokes.setup: Marge noticing Bart seems troubled
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete non-sequitur about glasses Bart never had
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - completely missing the point with impossible logic

jokes.text: I guess we could get more involved in Bart's activities. But then I'd be afraid of smothering him. - Yeah. And then we'd get the chair. - That's not what I meant. It was, Marge. Admit it.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about being overprotective
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks she means literally smothering leading to execution
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing Homer's literal interpretation of parenting concerns

jokes.text: Save me from the wee turtles! They were too quick for me!
jokes.setup: Willie claiming to save turtles
jokes.punchline: Willie being overwhelmed by tiny turtles
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Willie's typical incompetence

jokes.text: She's like a Milk Dud, Lis: Sweet on the outside, poison on the inside.
jokes.setup: Bart describing Jessica's deceptive nature
jokes.punchline: Candy metaphor that doesn't quite work logically
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor attempt that's slightly off, fitting for Bart's imperfect wisdom

jokes.text: Do you have Go, Dog, Go? / That's in Juvenile. This is Young Adult. / Well-read and just a little wild. If only someone could tame him.
jokes.setup: Library boy correcting book location
jokes.punchline: Lisa's romantic fantasy about 'taming' the librarian
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa character moment - romanticizing the most mundane interaction possible

jokes.text: Oh, Lis, she's already drawn me to her with her beautiful siren song.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to resist Jessica's influence
jokes.punchline: Jessica's 'siren song' is horrible screeching/singing
jokes.explanation: Good visual/audio gag subverting Bart's romantic description with awful reality

jokes.text: You're turning me into a criminal when all I want to be is a petty thug.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to break up with Jessica
jokes.punchline: Distinction between 'criminal' and 'petty thug' as if one is better
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart line showing his moral compass - he has standards, they're just very low

jokes.text: Thirty cents off Shake N' Bake. / Homer! / We can spare it, Marge. We've been blessed.
jokes.setup: Homer putting coupon in collection plate
jokes.punchline: His sincere belief they've been blessed enough to afford the charity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - genuine generosity with completely inappropriate offering

jokes.text: What is it? A Unitarian?
jokes.setup: Reverend asking congregation to look at something shocking
jokes.punchline: Suggestion that Unitarians are the most shocking thing imaginable in this church
jokes.explanation: Sharp religious humor about denominational prejudice, very economical

jokes.text: How convenient.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming Bart's fancy clothes are what he wore to church
jokes.punchline: Reverend's sarcastic response to obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcasm, serviceable but not exceptional

jokes.text: And you're just yellow trash.
jokes.setup: Jessica's threat to Bart about not being believed
jokes.punchline: Racial slur adapted for yellow cartoon characters
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of real-world prejudice to cartoon physics, shows Jessica's true nature

jokes.text: She's got a 108 IQ, she reads at a fifth-grade level. And her hair smells like red Froot Loops.
jokes.setup: Bart describing Jessica as criminal mastermind
jokes.punchline: List ending with absurdly specific and irrelevant detail about hair scent
jokes.explanation: Great escalation ending with wonderfully specific and irrelevant detail showing Bart's infatuation

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, I eat Froot Loops for breakfast.
jokes.setup: Bart describing Jessica's Froot Loops hair smell
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact response missing the point
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa logical literalness in response to Bart's romantic description

jokes.text: I smelled some marijuana smoke in Vietnam! / I was the one that canceled Star Trek! / I left my Porsche keys inside Mrs. Glick.
jokes.setup: Lisa's call for guilty person to confess
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and inappropriate confessions from congregation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with absurd confessions ranging from mundane to bizarre to nonsensical

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Smells like church.
jokes.setup: Finding the collection money
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's expert detective observation about church money
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum moment - his useless but confident police observations

jokes.text: Bart Simpson has somehow managed to sneak his bedroom into my house.
jokes.setup: Evidence found in Jessica's room
jokes.punchline: Jessica's absurd explanation blaming Bart for impossible crime
jokes.explanation: Good Jessica character moment - creative lying shows her manipulative nature

jokes.text: I was expelled, Dad. Remember the pipe bomb? The glee club brawl? Remember the school chapel collection plate? The exploding toilets ring a bell? Come on, Dad, pay attention to me!
jokes.setup: Jessica confessing to theft
jokes.punchline: Revealing extensive history of escalating crimes for attention
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list revealing Jessica's true criminal nature and daddy issues

jokes.text: Wait till she sees the second-rate job I do on these stairs.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming he won't be manipulated anymore
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing he's still under Jessica's control
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing Bart's self-deception about breaking free from manipulation
